five_questions.problem: LLMs need task-specific memory extraction (personalization, problem-solving, agentic tasks) but existing methods fail across heterogeneous tasks.
five_questions.hard: Conflicting gradients from mixed task types degrade self-evolving frameworks' performance.
five_questions.tried: GEPA, ACE, and MemEvolve frameworks that optimize prompts iteratively but fail on task diversity.
five_questions.outcomes: CluE achieves +9.04% relative gain via cluster-based evolution on BEHEMOTH benchmark.
five_questions.next: Explore alternative clustering methods (hierarchical/density-based) to improve robustness.

grounding_issues: A lightweight memory module lifts macro accuracy from 37.84% to 46.00%
